title: HTTP Protocol Engineering in Go 1.26: Bytes to HTTP/3
description: Go service developers who can build production HTTP clients and servers and now need byte-level protocol, parser, interoperability, and security mastery. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate go 1.26.5 octet and stream handling, partial i/o, tcp lifecycle, strict bounded http/1.1 parsing and serialization, request targets, fields, framing, fixed and chunked content, trailers, response control data, persistence, expectations, intermediaries, authority, tls 1.3, alpn, upgrade, connect, request smuggling and response splitting defense, slow-client limits, state machines, differential and fuzz testing, http/2 frames and hpack, http/3 quic and qpack, deployment, and incident defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-go protocol decision models plus explicit compilation, net.pipe, loopback socket, tls, proxy, packet-capture, fuzz, race, load, http/2, http/3, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative protocol-engineering projects and a performance-based certification exam through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Go, HTTP client and server, accessibility, security, testing, context, cleanup, and evidence habits before adding one protocol boundary.

Browser Go is deterministic and isolated. It models octets, framing, state, policy, and changed cases but never opens sockets, negotiates TLS, contacts networks or proxies, captures packets, executes host commands, reads host state, or handles credentials.

Passing work requires exact bytes or state input, a named invariant and owner, bounded resource and time policy, observable changed and failure results, cleanup and recovery, and explicit full-toolchain transfer limits.

competencies.statement: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
misconceptions: Successfully serving one browser request proves a protocol implementation is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.

competencies.statement: Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
misconceptions: An HTTP message maps one-to-one onto a TCP packet and arrives as one socket read.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.

competencies.statement: Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
misconceptions: The behavior of one popular server overrides normative protocol requirements.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.

competencies.statement: Separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions.
misconceptions: A parser is more interoperable when it accepts every malformed variation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate required conformance, optional tolerance, local policy, registered extensions, and unsupported behavior in parser decisions.

competencies.statement: Plan deterministic browser models, Go compilation, net.Pipe tests, loopback sockets, TLS, packet capture, proxy, fuzz, race, load, and production transfer gates.
misconceptions: Pure string parsing in a browser proves live socket, TLS, proxy, and production behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan deterministic browser models, Go compilation, net.Pipe tests, loopback sockets, TLS, packet capture, proxy, fuzz, race, load, and production transfer gates.

competencies.statement: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.
misconceptions: Go strings guarantee protocol input is valid UTF-8 text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.

competencies.statement: Interpret Read results using both byte count and error, including data with io.EOF, zero-byte reads, and temporary fragmentation.
misconceptions: Any non-nil read error means returned bytes must be discarded.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret Read results using both byte count and error, including data with io.EOF, zero-byte reads, and temporary fragmentation.

competencies.statement: Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.
misconceptions: A Writer either writes the entire slice or returns an error, so retry loops are unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.

competencies.statement: Use bufio.Reader without losing peeked, unread, or already-buffered bytes when protocol phases change.
misconceptions: Replacing a buffered reader around the same connection preserves all unread bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bufio.Reader without losing peeked, unread, or already-buffered bytes when protocol phases change.

competencies.statement: Prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence.
misconceptions: A parser that passes one whole-buffer test is independent of network fragmentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence.

competencies.statement: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.
misconceptions: TCP preserves application write boundaries and delivers each Write through one matching Read.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.

competencies.statement: Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.
misconceptions: Closing the listener automatically closes every accepted connection and handler goroutine.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.

competencies.statement: Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
misconceptions: A connection deadline is a relative duration that automatically refreshes after progress.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish orderly EOF, CloseRead, CloseWrite, full close, reset, timeout, and local shutdown effects on protocol completeness.
misconceptions: Receiving EOF always means the current HTTP message completed successfully.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish orderly EOF, CloseRead, CloseWrite, full close, reset, timeout, and local shutdown effects on protocol completeness.

competencies.statement: Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.
misconceptions: A failed socket write proves the peer received none of the bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.

competencies.statement: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.
misconceptions: HTTP semantics are defined by the exact capitalization and line layout of HTTP/1.1.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate version-neutral method, status, field, content, and resource semantics from HTTP/1.1 textual serialization.

competencies.statement: Model control data, header fields, content, trailer fields, and message metadata without inventing content where semantics forbid it.
misconceptions: Every request and response contains a body after its header section.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model control data, header fields, content, trailer fields, and message metadata without inventing content where semantics forbid it.

competencies.statement: Determine content meaning and permission from request method, response status, request context, and connection mode rather than one field alone.
misconceptions: Content-Length greater than zero guarantees a response carries content for every method and status.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Determine content meaning and permission from request method, response status, request context, and connection mode rather than one field alone.

competencies.statement: Interpret HTTP version as capability and conformance information while rejecting unsupported major versions and unsafe downgrade assumptions.
misconceptions: A higher minor HTTP version requires rejecting all messages from older peers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret HTTP version as capability and conformance information while rejecting unsupported major versions and unsafe downgrade assumptions.

competencies.statement: Trace origin, proxy, gateway, tunnel, cache, and user-agent transformations while retaining end-to-end and hop evidence.
misconceptions: An intermediary forwards the original request bytes unchanged to the origin.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace origin, proxy, gateway, tunnel, cache, and user-agent transformations while retaining end-to-end and hop evidence.

competencies.statement: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
misconceptions: ABNF alone expresses every semantic and security requirement in an HTTP specification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.

competencies.statement: Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
misconceptions: Trimming a parsed token after acceptance makes any surrounding bytes harmless.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.

competencies.statement: Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.
misconceptions: Accepting bare LF everywhere is required for HTTP/1.1 interoperability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.

competencies.statement: Bound start line, each field line, total fields, field count, content, trailers, states, allocations, and parse time before consuming them.
misconceptions: A maximum total request size alone prevents all parser memory and CPU exhaustion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound start line, each field line, total fields, field count, content, trailers, states, allocations, and parse time before consuming them.

competencies.statement: Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.
misconceptions: Calling application code while each line arrives reduces latency without changing security.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.

competencies.statement: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
misconceptions: Splitting a request line on arbitrary whitespace is equivalent to the strict HTTP/1.1 grammar.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.

competencies.statement: Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.
misconceptions: A parser should reject every syntactically valid method it does not already know.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.

competencies.statement: Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.
misconceptions: Every HTTP/1.1 request target begins with a slash and names an origin path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.

competencies.statement: Preserve raw target evidence while parsing URI components, percent encodings, query delimiters, and canonical routing values without double decoding.
misconceptions: Percent-decoding the entire request target before splitting components is always safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve raw target evidence while parsing URI components, percent encodings, query delimiters, and canonical routing values without double decoding.

competencies.statement: Test unknown methods, empty targets, fragments, userinfo, encoded delimiters, invalid versions, excess whitespace, and bounded lengths.
misconceptions: A request line parser is complete after GET slash HTTP slash one-point-one succeeds.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test unknown methods, empty targets, fragments, userinfo, encoded delimiters, invalid versions, excess whitespace, and bounded lengths.

competencies.statement: Parse case-insensitive token field names, colon delimiters, optional field whitespace, and field values while rejecting whitespace before the colon.
misconceptions: Whitespace before a field colon is cosmetic and can be trimmed safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse case-insensitive token field names, colon delimiters, optional field whitespace, and field values while rejecting whitespace before the colon.

competencies.statement: Preserve field-line order and repeated values, combining only when that field definition permits it.
misconceptions: Every repeated HTTP field can be joined with a comma without changing meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve field-line order and repeated values, combining only when that field definition permits it.

competencies.statement: Parse Connection options and nominate hop-by-hop fields dynamically instead of relying only on a fixed list.
misconceptions: Removing the standard hop-by-hop names is sufficient even when Connection names more fields.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse Connection options and nominate hop-by-hop fields dynamically instead of relying only on a fixed list.

competencies.statement: Parse RFC 9651 items, lists, dictionaries, parameters, numbers, strings, tokens, bytes, booleans, dates, and display strings strictly when a field opts in.
misconceptions: Every HTTP field uses Structured Fields syntax and can be parsed by one generic comma splitter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse RFC 9651 items, lists, dictionaries, parameters, numbers, strings, tokens, bytes, booleans, dates, and display strings strictly when a field opts in.

competencies.statement: Bound field bytes and count, reject obsolete folding and controls, redact secrets, and preserve only safe diagnostic evidence.
misconceptions: Logging every raw field is necessary for parser debugging and has no security cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound field bytes and count, reject obsolete folding and controls, redact secrets, and preserve only safe diagnostic evidence.

competencies.statement: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.
misconceptions: Content-Length always determines message framing whenever the field appears.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.

competencies.statement: Forbid content framing for HEAD responses, 1xx, 204, successful CONNECT, and other context-specific cases while retaining metadata meaning.
misconceptions: A positive Content-Length means a 204 response includes that many content bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Forbid content framing for HEAD responses, 1xx, 204, successful CONNECT, and other context-specific cases while retaining metadata meaning.

competencies.statement: Validate transfer-coding order, require final chunked coding for request framing, and close after ambiguous Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length input.
misconceptions: Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length can be used together safely when both predict the same size.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate transfer-coding order, require final chunked coding for request framing, and close after ambiguous Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length input.

competencies.statement: Parse decimal Content-Length values without overflow and accept repeated or list values only when every valid value is identical.
misconceptions: Selecting the first Content-Length value is safe when later values differ.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse decimal Content-Length values without overflow and accept repeated or list values only when every valid value is identical.

competencies.statement: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.
misconceptions: A valid start line and field section prove the complete message is safe to dispatch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

competencies.statement: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.
misconceptions: A validated Content-Length is safe to use directly as a slice allocation length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.

competencies.statement: Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.
misconceptions: EOF terminates any HTTP content successfully regardless of expected length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.

competencies.statement: Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.
misconceptions: Reading until no bytes are immediately available is equivalent to reading one HTTP body.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.

competencies.statement: Use close-delimited response content only in permitted response contexts and mark the connection non-reusable.
misconceptions: Requests without Content-Length are safely framed by closing the connection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use close-delimited response content only in permitted response contexts and mark the connection non-reusable.

competencies.statement: Keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks.
misconceptions: A small framed gzip body guarantees a small decoded representation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks.

competencies.statement: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.
misconceptions: Chunk sizes use decimal notation and can be parsed with ordinary base-ten conversion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.

competencies.statement: Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
misconceptions: A short read at a chunk boundary means the sender finished that chunk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.

competencies.statement: Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
misconceptions: The zero chunk ends the message before any trailer section is parsed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.

competencies.statement: Validate trailer declarations and forbidden fields, retain trailers separately, and merge only when the field definition explicitly permits.
misconceptions: Trailer fields can always overwrite corresponding header fields after content arrives.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate trailer declarations and forbidden fields, retain trailers separately, and merge only when the field definition explicitly permits.

competencies.statement: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.
misconceptions: One multi-chunk happy path proves a chunked decoder preserves every message boundary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.

competencies.statement: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.
misconceptions: The reason phrase defines response semantics and must match a standard English phrase.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.

competencies.statement: Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
misconceptions: The first received status line always begins the final response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.

competencies.statement: Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
misconceptions: A HEAD response with Content-Length must consume that many content bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.

competencies.statement: Transition a successful CONNECT response from HTTP message parsing to opaque tunnel bytes without losing buffered data.
misconceptions: A successful CONNECT response is followed by an ordinary HTTP response body.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transition a successful CONNECT response from HTTP message parsing to opaque tunnel bytes without losing buffered data.

competencies.statement: Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.
misconceptions: No final response means the server certainly did not process the request.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.

competencies.statement: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
misconceptions: A later status line can replace an earlier one after response content starts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.

competencies.statement: Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
misconceptions: Escaping newline characters after concatenation prevents every response-splitting attack.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.

competencies.statement: Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
misconceptions: Chunked encoding is always the best response framing for HTTP/1.1.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.

competencies.statement: Drive all header and content bytes through a checked full-write loop and retain an exact bounded transcript for tests.
misconceptions: bytes.Buffer tests prove a serializer handles short network writes correctly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Drive all header and content bytes through a checked full-write loop and retain an exact bounded transcript for tests.

competencies.statement: Round-trip supported messages through independent parse and serialize paths while preserving semantics, limits, and declared normalization.
misconceptions: Byte-for-byte round-trip identity is required even for field-name case and permitted whitespace normalization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Round-trip supported messages through independent parse and serialize paths while preserving semantics, limits, and declared normalization.

competencies.statement: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.
misconceptions: Every HTTP/1.1 connection remains reusable until the peer closes it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.

competencies.statement: Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
misconceptions: A server can ignore an unread request body and immediately parse the next start line safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.

competencies.statement: Preserve request-response ordering across pipelined HTTP/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order.
misconceptions: HTTP/1.1 response order may follow handler completion because each response carries an identifier.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve request-response ordering across pipelined HTTP/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order.

competencies.statement: Bound requests per connection, idle time, lifetime, buffered residual data, and closure reasons without truncating active messages.
misconceptions: One generous idle timeout protects equally against slow clients and resource retention.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound requests per connection, idle time, lifetime, buffered residual data, and closure reasons without truncating active messages.

competencies.statement: Close and discard connection state after ambiguous framing, parse failure, incomplete output, unexpected residual bytes, or state disagreement.
misconceptions: Returning 400 is enough to resynchronize a persistent connection after malformed framing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close and discard connection state after ambiguous framing, parse failure, incomplete output, unexpected residual bytes, or state disagreement.

competencies.statement: Parse Expect, support 100-continue deliberately, and return appropriate final failure for unsupported expectations.
misconceptions: Any Expect field requires sending status 100 before validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse Expect, support 100-continue deliberately, and return appropriate final failure for unsupported expectations.

competencies.statement: Send 100 Continue only after header-level policy accepts receiving content and before a final response commits.
misconceptions: A server may send 100 Continue after it has already sent a final rejection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Send 100 Continue only after header-level policy accepts receiving content and before a final response commits.

competencies.statement: Bound client waiting for 100, handle early final responses, and avoid sending content after rejection.
misconceptions: A client must wait forever for 100 Continue before sending its request body.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound client waiting for 100, handle early final responses, and avoid sending content after rejection.

competencies.statement: Choose bounded drain versus connection close after early rejection based on remaining framing, risk, reuse value, and deadline.
misconceptions: Fully draining every rejected body is always safer than closing the connection.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose bounded drain versus connection close after early rejection based on remaining framing, risk, reuse value, and deadline.

competencies.statement: Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.
misconceptions: A happy-path continue exchange proves both peers avoid coordination deadlocks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.

competencies.statement: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
misconceptions: Hop-by-hop fields are a fixed list that never depends on the Connection field value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.

competencies.statement: Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.
misconceptions: A reverse proxy transparently extends the client TCP connection to the origin.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.

competencies.statement: Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.
misconceptions: Changing representation bytes does not affect HTTP metadata if the media type stays the same.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.

competencies.statement: Construct trusted Via and forwarding evidence at controlled hops without accepting client-supplied identity as authoritative.
misconceptions: Appending one trusted value makes all earlier X-Forwarded values trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct trusted Via and forwarding evidence at controlled hops without accepting client-supplied identity as authoritative.

competencies.statement: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.
misconceptions: Every failure observed by a proxy should be reported as an origin status 500.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.

competencies.statement: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.
misconceptions: The TCP destination address supplies HTTP authority, so Host is optional in HTTP/1.1.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.

competencies.statement: Reconstruct the effective request URI from target form, request-target, Host, connection security, and trusted configuration.
misconceptions: The request-target alone always contains the complete effective URI.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconstruct the effective request URI from target form, request-target, Host, connection security, and trusted configuration.

competencies.statement: Handle proxy absolute-form authority and Host according to RFC rules while rejecting unsafe disagreement.
misconceptions: When absolute-form and Host disagree, whichever appears first should control routing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle proxy absolute-form authority and Host according to RFC rules while rejecting unsafe disagreement.

competencies.statement: Normalize host and port only as specified, validate against configured authority, then route, cache, authorize tenants, or construct links.
misconceptions: A syntactically valid authority is safe for password-reset links and tenant routing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Normalize host and port only as specified, validate against configured authority, then route, cache, authorize tenants, or construct links.

competencies.statement: Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.
misconceptions: Matching SNI and Host proves the authenticated user and intended application tenant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish connection IP, TLS SNI, certificate identity, ALPN, HTTP Host or authority, and application tenant evidence.

competencies.statement: Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.
misconceptions: TLS preserves HTTP message boundaries and turns each HTTP message into one TLS record.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.

competencies.statement: Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.
misconceptions: A certificate signed by any trusted root is valid for every requested hostname.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.

competencies.statement: Offer and select application protocols with ALPN before application data and reject unsupported or inconsistent negotiation.
misconceptions: A server can inspect the first HTTP request to decide retroactively whether ALPN selected h2.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Offer and select application protocols with ALPN before application data and reject unsupported or inconsistent negotiation.

competencies.statement: Define supported versions, cipher and curve policy, session resumption, client authentication, and replay boundaries while treating early data explicitly.
misconceptions: TLS session resumption proves a replayed application request is new.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define supported versions, cipher and curve policy, session resumption, client authentication, and replay boundaries while treating early data explicitly.

competencies.statement: State that TLS protects a connection hop but does not provide application authorization, safe methods, trusted intermediaries, malware safety, or end-to-end storage secrecy.
misconceptions: HTTPS alone makes every request authorized and every received payload safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State that TLS protects a connection hop but does not provide application authorization, safe methods, trusted intermediaries, malware safety, or end-to-end storage secrecy.

competencies.statement: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
misconceptions: An Upgrade request authorizes the client to send the new protocol immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.

competencies.statement: Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
misconceptions: CONNECT may target any supplied host and port because the server only transports bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.

competencies.statement: Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.
misconceptions: Changing parser functions is enough; bufio cannot hold bytes beyond the HTTP message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.

competencies.statement: Apply RFC 9931 by forbidding unsafe optimistic new-protocol bytes in HTTP/1.1 until transition acceptance is known.
misconceptions: Completing the transition request guarantees the server accepted the new protocol.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply RFC 9931 by forbidding unsafe optimistic new-protocol bytes in HTTP/1.1 until transition acceptance is known.

competencies.statement: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.
misconceptions: Every rejected Upgrade or CONNECT must close the connection even when no optimistic bytes were sent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.

competencies.statement: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
misconceptions: CL.TE and TE.CL ambiguity is harmless when the local parser chooses one framing rule consistently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.

competencies.statement: Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
misconceptions: Normalizing malformed whitespace before forwarding removes all request-smuggling risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.

competencies.statement: Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
misconceptions: If two parsers produce the same visible path, they necessarily enforce the same request boundary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.

competencies.statement: Prevent attacker-controlled CRLF and control bytes from creating new fields, responses, logs, caches, or downstream protocol records.
misconceptions: HTML escaping is sufficient to prevent HTTP response splitting.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent attacker-controlled CRLF and control bytes from creating new fields, responses, logs, caches, or downstream protocol records.

competencies.statement: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
misconceptions: A safe HTTP/2 front end guarantees the translated HTTP/1.1 backend request cannot be ambiguous.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.

competencies.statement: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
misconceptions: Refreshing a read deadline after every byte defeats slow-client resource exhaustion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.

competencies.statement: Bound per-connection buffers, retained slices, field maps, content queues, trailer data, and pooled-object capacity before allocation.
misconceptions: A bounded content body guarantees total memory per connection is bounded.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound per-connection buffers, retained slices, field maps, content queues, trailer data, and pooled-object capacity before allocation.

competencies.statement: Propagate slow peer writes through bounded queues, cancellation, deadlines, partial output evidence, and producer shutdown.
misconceptions: Buffering all response bytes in memory eliminates backpressure problems.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate slow peer writes through bounded queues, cancellation, deadlines, partial output evidence, and producer shutdown.

competencies.statement: Limit connections, active parsers, work, tenant share, and output while rejecting early and preserving fairness and health evidence.
misconceptions: A single global connection limit guarantees fair service for every tenant and request cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Limit connections, active parsers, work, tenant share, and output while rejecting early and preserving fairness and health evidence.

competencies.statement: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.
misconceptions: Defers guarantee cleanup finishes within budget after every timeout and forced shutdown.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.

competencies.statement: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
misconceptions: A parser that returns need-more-data has no state worth testing separately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.

competencies.statement: Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
misconceptions: Valid output examples are stronger evidence than invariants over every parser transition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.

competencies.statement: Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.
misconceptions: Multiple goroutines may read one TCP stream safely because each request has a separate boundary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.

competencies.statement: Arbitrate parse, application, peer, timeout, cancellation, write, and shutdown failures so exactly one terminal decision owns response and cleanup.
misconceptions: The last goroutine to report an error should choose the connection outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Arbitrate parse, application, peer, timeout, cancellation, write, and shutdown failures so exactly one terminal decision owns response and cleanup.

competencies.statement: Record low-cardinality phase, framing source, bytes, duration, terminal reason, peer class, and correlation without raw secrets or attacker-controlled labels.
misconceptions: Logging the entire raw message is the only way to diagnose parser state failures.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record low-cardinality phase, framing source, bytes, duration, terminal reason, peer class, and correlation without raw secrets or attacker-controlled labels.

competencies.statement: Use exact byte fixtures for valid, incomplete, malformed, normalized, serialized, and residual-buffer cases with readable semantic assertions.
misconceptions: Golden bytes should replace semantic assertions because byte equality proves every behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use exact byte fixtures for valid, incomplete, malformed, normalized, serialized, and residual-buffer cases with readable semantic assertions.

competencies.statement: Use net.Pipe or a scripted short reader and writer to test fragmentation, blocking, deadlines, half-close, cancellation, and cleanup deterministically.
misconceptions: Loopback TCP reliably produces every fragmentation and short-write schedule needed for tests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use net.Pipe or a scripted short reader and writer to test fragmentation, blocking, deadlines, half-close, cancellation, and cleanup deterministically.

competencies.statement: Compare supported inputs with Go net/http and independent recipients while treating deliberate strictness and implementation quirks as reviewed differences.
misconceptions: Any difference from net/http means the custom parser is wrong, even when its documented policy is stricter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare supported inputs with Go net/http and independent recipients while treating deliberate strictness and implementation quirks as reviewed differences.

competencies.statement: Fuzz parser and serializer with seed corpora, fragmentation, invariants, round trips, bounds, no panic, no hang, and retained minimized failures.
misconceptions: A fuzz target is useful only when it discovers a crash rather than a semantic or resource invariant violation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fuzz parser and serializer with seed corpora, fragmentation, invariants, round trips, bounds, no panic, no hang, and retained minimized failures.

competencies.statement: Run race, leak, allocation, benchmark, profiling, and changed-load gates while separating correctness from capacity claims.
misconceptions: Passing the race detector and a fast microbenchmark proves production protocol safety and capacity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run race, leak, allocation, benchmark, profiling, and changed-load gates while separating correctness from capacity claims.

competencies.statement: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
misconceptions: HTTP/2 begins with an HTTP/1.1 request line followed by binary response frames.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.

competencies.statement: Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.
misconceptions: Closing one HTTP/2 stream closes the underlying TCP connection and every other stream.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.

competencies.statement: Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.
misconceptions: HPACK compression is stateless per request and cannot expose cross-stream information.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.

competencies.statement: Coordinate stream and connection flow control, frame size, concurrent streams, backpressure, fairness, reset, GOAWAY, and retry evidence.
misconceptions: TCP flow control alone prevents one HTTP/2 stream from consuming all application buffers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Coordinate stream and connection flow control, frame size, concurrent streams, backpressure, fairness, reset, GOAWAY, and retry evidence.

competencies.statement: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.
misconceptions: A training frame decoder should replace the maintained Go HTTP/2 stack in production.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.

competencies.statement: Distinguish QUIC connection IDs, encrypted packets, transport parameters, bidirectional and unidirectional streams, migration, loss recovery, and UDP path behavior.
misconceptions: QUIC is TCP over UDP and preserves the same connection and head-of-line behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish QUIC connection IDs, encrypted packets, transport parameters, bidirectional and unidirectional streams, migration, loss recovery, and UDP path behavior.

competencies.statement: Trace HTTP/3 control streams, SETTINGS, request streams, frame placement, cancellation, GOAWAY, and connection versus stream errors.
misconceptions: HTTP/3 sends every request and response through one ordered application stream.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace HTTP/3 control streams, SETTINGS, request streams, frame placement, cancellation, GOAWAY, and connection versus stream errors.

competencies.statement: Explain QPACK encoder and decoder streams, dynamic-table capacity, blocked streams, acknowledgements, confidentiality, and decompression limits.
misconceptions: QPACK is identical to HPACK because both compress field sections.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain QPACK encoder and decoder streams, dynamic-table capacity, blocked streams, acknowledgements, confidentiality, and decompression limits.

competencies.statement: Stage Alt-Svc discovery, certificate and authority checks, version negotiation, fallback, cache lifetime, disablement, and rollback without breaking HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2.
misconceptions: Advertising h3 causes every client to switch immediately and permanently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stage Alt-Svc discovery, certificate and authority checks, version negotiation, fallback, cache lifetime, disablement, and rollback without breaking HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2.

competencies.statement: Defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, TLS identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk.
misconceptions: A green HTTP/3 smoke request proves all protocol versions, proxies, clients, and failure paths are production ready.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend standards scope, byte parsing, framing, transitions, TLS identity, intermediaries, limits, state invariants, tests, observability, version negotiation, deployment, rollback, and residual risk.

credential.title: HTTP Protocol Engineering in Go 1.26: Bytes to HTTP/3 Certificate
